old_claim.note: Renewable Energy Systems provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Wind generation supplied the renewable electricity for the Utsira milestone that anchors this node.

source_shape.source_locator: Hydrogen-storage section and Figure 16.2 identify Utsira as the world's first-of-a-kind 2004 wind-hydrogen demonstration project, using a wind farm, electrolyser, hydrogen storage, and conversion equipment.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The chronology anchor produced hydrogen directly from wind electricity in a first-of-a-kind full-scale system.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The Utsira milestone required wind generation but was a stand-alone system, so it did not require the distinct utility-grid-integration framework represented by renewable_energy_systems.

ontology_before: Green hydrogen depended on broad utility-grid integration even though renewable electrolysis can operate as a stand-alone coupled system.
ontology_after: The first full-scale green-hydrogen milestone depends directly on its documented wind-power source, while broader grid integration is absent.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-grid-battery-green-hydrogen-boundary: wind_power_modern is required and renewable_energy_systems is absent.
would_reject_if: The chronology anchor is changed from Utsira to a grid-integrated multi-renewable hydrogen system.
Reliable evidence shows Utsira's electrolysis was not powered by wind generation.
short_reason: Utsira directly coupled wind power to electrolysis and did not require utility-grid renewable integration.
